recent_calls.summary: The user, Betty, called to reschedule her appointment to June 6th after 3 PM. The agent noted the request and informed her that a representative would call back on the next business day to confirm. The call ended with no further requests from the user.

The user called to confirm an appointment but the agent informed them that appointment confirmations cannot be done after hours. The agent assured the user that the team will follow up on the next business day to confirm the appointment. The user did not request any additional assistance.

The user reported that their new furnace and air conditioning are not turning on, describing it as a comfort issue with no emergency present. The agent collected details and scheduled a morning appointment window for a technician visit, promising a follow-up the next business day. The call concluded with confirmation of the service request and no additional instructions from the user.

Jeanique from American Home Shield called Budget's after-hours line to inquire about the soonest appointment date for a work order and requested a callback to confirm if transferring the appointment is acceptable. The agent took down the details and informed Jeanique that the team will follow up on the next business day with the requested information.

Christopher called to schedule an emergency cooling service because his cooling system is not coming on. The agent collected his service address and confirmed the emergency status, then documented and prioritized the request for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from Christopher.

Elaine called to report that her air conditioner fan is not blowing properly and requested service for Monday morning at her address in Florissant, Missouri. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. Elaine declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Jay from American Home Shield called Budget's after-hours line to report an issue with a forced air furnace where the fan won't come on. The agent took down the service request details, confirmed the service address and callback number, and scheduled the soonest available appointment during business hours. The call ended with the agent offering maintenance program information, which Jay declined.

The user called after receiving a missed call regarding a service request. The agent explained that scheduling cannot be done after hours but offered to update and pass along the service request to the team. The call ended abruptly before the user could provide further details.
The user briefly answered the call but did not engage in a meaningful conversation or request assistance before hanging up.

Gina called to report that her air conditioning unit is not blowing air and has ice buildup both outside and around the furnace, with concern for her 93-year-old mother who has health issues. The agent documented the issue, marked it as urgent due to the health concerns, and escalated it for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended with the agent confirming contact details and assuring Gina that the team will reach out soon.

Ariel from American Home Shield Escalation called to confirm the property address and understand why a dispatch was canceled. The after-hours agent, Evie, documented the request and informed Ariel that a representative would contact them on the next business day. The call ended with no further questions from Ariel.

The user reported a routine issue with their air conditioner making loud and knocking noises and briefly cutting off earlier, though it is currently working. They requested a service appointment for Monday, confirmed no special access instructions, and provided their contact and service address details. The agent confirmed the information and assured the user that the team will reach out to confirm the appointment.

The user called to cancel an appointment scheduled for Wednesday. The agent confirmed the cancellation request and informed the user that the team would follow up to confirm. The call ended politely with no further requests from the user.

The user called to inquire about the schedule for their AC installation after sending the necessary documentation and signing with Synchrony. The agent confirmed the user's details and then transferred the call to a live agent who could assist with the installation schedule.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to inquire about their next appointment and was informed about a past appointment. The user requested a new appointment and expressed frustration about not being contacted after scheduling. The call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

Kenneth Peabody called to inquire about warranty coverage for a previous air conditioning service and to report that his air conditioner is blowing hot air again. The agent confirmed that Budget still works with American Home Shield and scheduled a service appointment for the next available time. Kenneth declined information about maintenance programs but provided his work order number and gave positive feedback about the call experience.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.
The user called and requested to speak to Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user to the requested person and successfully transferred the call.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user called to speak with a representative regarding payment. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

Carlotta contacted Budget to schedule a service appointment for her air conditioner, which was blowing air but not cooling. The agent booked a provisional appointment for Monday, June 1st at 12 PM and confirmed the details with Carlotta. The user was satisfied with the quick response and courteous service, providing positive feedback.

The user called to get the email address to send a picture to Budget. The agent provided the email address info@justcallbudget.com and repeated it several times to ensure clarity. The call ended with the user confirming the email and thanking the agent.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to transfer the call to the requested person and successfully completed the transfer.

The user called to request a same-day service for their AC system, which stopped working due to a faulty thermostat installation by Vivint. The agent collected all necessary information, confirmed the details with the user, and informed them that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them soon. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.

The user, Terryzena, called to schedule an air conditioning service under their American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the details, including the issue of the AC not cooling and the preferred appointment time of Monday morning. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback on the AI assistant.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent's initial greeting and request for the user's ZIP code. The user did not respond before hanging up.

The user called to request a repair service for their water heater due to no hot water. The agent provided available appointment slots for June 1st and 2nd, but the user insisted on a same-day appointment, which was not available. The call ended without scheduling an appointment.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.
The user returned a call they had received earlier and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.

The user called to inquire about purchasing a warranty for their AC units under Budget warranty. The agent gathered initial information and then transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user called Budget for service assistance in Illinois and requested to speak with a representative. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further help.

Oraine called Budget to request service for a non-working upstairs central air conditioning unit, referred by American Home Shield. The agent collected necessary details, confirmed the request, and informed Oraine that someone from Budget would contact her soon. Oraine expressed interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the call experience.

Aaron called to report that the air conditioning at his rental property in Hazelwood stopped working last night, causing a comfort issue for tenants. He requested the soonest available service appointment, and a provisional booking was made for Monday, June 1st at 10 AM. The agent confirmed the details and informed Aaron about the dispatch fee, and the call ended with Aaron declining to provide feedback.

The user called to confirm if their voicemail regarding an expedited dispatch request was received. The agent acknowledged the dispatch number and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance with the expedited service.

Sheryl from American Home Shield called to request expedited air conditioning service at 29 Grimmer Avenue, Edwardsville, due to a health condition of the resident's mother. The agent confirmed all details, including contact information and urgency, and informed Sheryl that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would reach out shortly. Sheryl declined additional information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback on the call experience.

The user called to set up a cooling service request because their AC unit is completely out. The agent collected the user's name, address, phone number, and preferred service time, and confirmed the details before ending the call. The user declined to provide an email for updates.

The user reported a heating and cooling unit not cooling in room twelve at the Center For Autism and provided the correct service address. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled a service follow-up for the next business day. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user, Mitch Ludluff, requested a quote for a new HVAC system due to a refrigerant leak in their 25-year-old unit. The agent collected the user's contact information and service address, and confirmed that the user wants service as soon as possible. The agent informed Mitch that the team will reach out the next business day to confirm availability.

The user called to clarify conflicting appointment emails about visits scheduled for the evening and June third. The agent acknowledged the confusion, documented the concern, and assured the user that the team would review and follow up the next business day. The user confirmed availability for the evening visit if it is confirmed and requested a text or call when the visitor is on the way.

The user called Budget after hours, confused about the office hours, and questioned if the agent was a real person. The agent explained the after-hours support and offered to take the user's information for a follow-up on the next business day, but the call ended shortly after without further details.
The call was an inbound after-hours call where the agent, Evie, greeted the user Hannah and asked if she needed assistance or a follow-up. The user did not respond, and the call ended shortly after.

Danny from American Home Shield called to request an expedited service dispatch for an urgent air conditioning issue related to a health concern. The agent documented the request, escalated it for next business day follow-up, and confirmed callback details. The user requested a callback within thirty minutes if the work order could be accepted on an expedited basis.

The user called after hours requesting to speak with a live representative. The agent informed the user that live representatives were unavailable but offered to take their information for a follow-up on the next business day.

The user called to inquire about the service charge for an AC unit. The agent explained that exact pricing was not available but offered to have the team contact the user with details and promotions. The user declined to leave contact information, and the call ended without further action.

The user, Tina, called to reschedule an appointment she could not attend at the originally scheduled time. The agent informed her that live transfers were unavailable after 4 PM but documented her request and assured a representative would contact her on the next business day to confirm the new appointment. Tina provided a preferred time frame of next week and ended the call after confirming no further assistance was needed.

Danny from American Home Shield called to inquire about accepting a work order on an expedited basis. The agent asked if the request was an emergency or could be handled during a regular appointment, but the call ended shortly after without further information.

Mary called to inquire about how to pay for the Savings Club membership after approving the estimate online. The agent explained the payment process and offered to have the team follow up with a payment link, which Mary accepted. The call ended with the agent confirming the follow-up and offering further assistance if needed.

Julie called Budget's after-hours service to follow up on an appointment request for her air conditioner made earlier that morning, as she had not received a callback. The agent apologized for the delay, documented her request, and informed her that a team member would contact her on the next business day. Julie also inquired about Saturday hours, which the agent provided.
